title: 11 New Hydrogen Projects Accelerate Development of Sustainable Hydrogen
content: Eleven new projects involving large electrolyzers are underway, with companies in the Netherlands providing subsidies to produce sustainable hydrogen. The electrolyzers have a combined capacity of approximately 602 megawatts (MW). Refineries, the chemical industry, and gas stations can use the hydrogen to further enhance sustainability and reduce CO2 emissions .The 602 MW electrolysis capacity is three times larger than the largest hydrogen plant currently under construction in the Netherlands. It's also enough to power 5,000 hydrogen-powered trucks for a year. The companies receiving subsidies will gain experience with the technology, permitting procedures, and obtaining certificates for their sustainable hydrogen. In this way, we aim to accelerate the development of sustainable hydrogen through subsidies.
author: Analysts Account
